factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: Fed rate-hike bets have faded sharply on US-Iran deal; 2y Treasury yield dropped to 4.02% on Monday per InvestingLive. Lower real yields directly support gold valuations.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy Trajectory
factors.summary: Market now pricing lower probability of near-term Fed hikes due to Iran deal reducing inflation/energy shock risk. FOMC decision Wednesday will be critical test; currently priced as hold at 3.75%.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse (Hormuz Deal)
factors.summary: US-Iran comprehensive framework to reopen Strait of Hormuz; Brent slashed by Goldman to $80 (2026) and $75 (2027); WTI near $80. Lower oil = lower inflation expectations = lower real yields = bullish gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY holding near 99.70 but hedging bets on weaker USD amid Iran deal; risk-on flows favor long bonds/equities/EM currencies over dollar. Softer dollar typically bullish for gold.

factors.name: BoJ Rate Hike to 1%
factors.summary: BoJ raised rates 25bp to 1% as expected; bond taper paused until April 2027. JPY strengthened initially but gains pared; USD/JPY near 160.10. Taper pause is dovish structural element, limiting immediate yen bullish impact.

factors.name: China Economic Data
factors.summary: China retail sales fell 0.6% YoY in May (first drop since pandemic per NBS); fixed asset investment contracted -4.1% YoY vs -2% expected. Weak demand weighs on growth expectations and commodity risk sentiment.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment / Equity Action
factors.summary: US equity futures rallied sharply on Iran deal (S&P 500 +1.7%, Nasdaq +3.1% overnight); risk appetite strong. In current environment, gold can benefit from both lower real yields AND risk-on flow rotation toward EM/commodities.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran Deal)
factors.summary: US-Iran peace memorandum removes major tail-risk premium in energy/FX markets; Strait of Hormuz reopening expected Friday per Trump. Lower geopolitical uncertainty typically allows gold to trade on technicals/real-yield basis rather than safe-haven bid.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No new CB gold buying flows mentioned in 24h news window; structural bid remains but not a near-term marginal driver.

factors.name: Upcoming FOMC Decision
factors.summary: Fed decision Wednesday; market consensus is hold at 3.75%. Outcome largely priced; major downside risk if Powell surprises with hawkish guidance, but base case is unchanged rates with softer forward tone given oil/inflation relief.

screening.narrative: Gold rallied modestly (+0.29% in 4h, +0.08% in 24h) following the US-Iran peace framework announcement and Strait of Hormuz reopening deal. The immediate driver is a structural repricing of inflation/energy risk lower: oil collapsed (Brent target slashed to $80–$75), real yields are compressing as Fed hike bets fade, and the US Dollar is softening on risk-on flows into EM equities and bonds. BoJ rate hike to 1% was priced in and met no surprise; the dovish taper-pause signal has limited the yen's upside. However, weak China data (first retail sales contraction since pandemic, -4.1% FAI) introduces growth headwinds that could eventually pressure risk appetite if the Iran dividend fades. The move is still in early-to-mid phase: gold has begun to climb, but it has not yet fully participated in the broader de-risking of inflation/rate assumptions.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI/Core CPI release (likely this week, exact date TBD from news)
Strait of Hormuz formal reopening (expected Friday per Trump)
Further US-Iran deal details and geopolitical reaction (Israel, Saudi Arabia)
BoJ Deputy Governor Uchida forward guidance signal (already released, but market parsing implications for July trajectory)
screening.risk_warning: If FOMC Wednesday surprises with hawkish hold language or dots signal higher terminal rates, real yields will spike and gold's rally could reverse sharply. If Iran deal negotiations collapse or geopolitical tensions reignite, oil will snap back and inflation expectations will re-anchor higher, benefiting real yields and USD at gold's expense. If China growth signals deteriorate further (hard landing risk), a risk-off flight to safety could steepen the 10y yield and favor USD/bonds over gold despite lower nominal rates. Equity weakness would also test whether gold's recent strength is sustainable or just a temporary risk-on move.
screening.entry_guidance: Gold is in PHASE_2 of the Iran-deal rally; entry for bulls should target a retest of $4,330–$4,340 support (minor pullback) before re-escalating. Avoid chasing breakout toward $4,380+ on this intraday action; wait for Fed decision Wednesday for clarity on real yield regime. Short-term traders should respect 4h close above $4,360 as a bullish break.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Deal (Geopolitical Relief)
factors.summary: Surprise US-Iran breakthrough has eased Middle East war premium; oil slumped to 3-month low (~$79.20 WTI), reducing inflation shock fears. Multiple sources confirm Strait of Hormuz reopening expected by Friday, removing supply-side inflation risk.

factors.name: Oil Price Collapse
factors.summary: WTI and Brent hit 3-month lows; lower energy prices reduce CPI/core inflation expectations, which in turn softens Fed hawkish repricing. Citi noted 'relief in sight' for inflation (Bloomberg 09:17).

factors.name: Real Yields (Implied Direction)
factors.summary: Lower oil → lower inflation expectations → lower nominal yields; real yields likely softening. However, FOMC decision tomorrow could reverse this if Fed surprises hawkish (per new Chair Warsh expectations of 'slight hawkish shift' — BNY strategists 06:32).

factors.name: BoJ Rate Hike (100 bps to 1.0%, highest since 1995)
factors.summary: BoJ's aggressive move signals global monetary tightening cycle still intact. However, ING notes BoJ policy remains 'accommodative' and real rates still deeply negative (JPY carry unwinding could lift USD temporarily, headwind for gold). USD/JPY near intervention threshold (160.25).

factors.name: RBA Holds at 4.35%, Hawkish Tone
factors.summary: RBA Governor Bullock stated 'inflation remains too high' and 'hikes are not off the table' despite pause. Prevents dovish surprise but confirms central banks remain vigilant on inflation.

factors.name: Fed Policy Ahead (FOMC Tomorrow)
factors.summary: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh expected to deliver 'slightly hawkish' statement shift per BNY strategists (06:32). Markets pricing in potential dot plot hawkish repricing, which would spike real yields and pressure gold. OCBC (08:15) notes gold upside 'dependent on softer Oil, lower yields and evidence Fed hawkish repricing has peaked' — not assured.

factors.name: US Dollar Index & Key Pairs
factors.summary: USD rallying despite oil collapse, supported by strong US data and Fed expectations (ING 08:22). EUR/USD failed to sustain above 1.1620, now 1.1590; USD/JPY near 160.25 intervention level. Strong dollar is headwind for gold.

factors.name: Gold Price Action (YTD + Intra-Session)
factors.summary: Gold rebounded ~5% as US-Iran de-escalation eased rate shock; up +0.56% in 24h and +0.52% in 4h. Move has already begun (Phase 2), suggesting relief trade partially priced in.

factors.name: Inflation Surprise Data
factors.summary: Italy CPI confirmed +3.2% y/y in May (vs +2.7% prior), upside surprise on energy; energy products +12.5% y/y (+9.5% prior). Counters pure 'disinflation' narrative but oil collapse should dominate forward path.

factors.name: German Sentiment Recovery
factors.summary: ZEW Outlook jumped to +10.5 vs -6.0 expected on Iran peace hopes, but Current Conditions fell to -81.0 vs -78.0 expected — mixed signal. Eurozone macro fragility remains.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No new announcements in 24h news window; structural PBoC/RBI demand remains steady baseline.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows
factors.signal: NEUTRAL (no specific holdings data in news)
factors.summary: No GLD/IAU flows reported in news window; cannot assess institutional short-covering or outflows.

screening.narrative: Gold rebounded ~5% following the surprise US-Iran peace deal, which triggered a sharp oil collapse (WTI to 3-month lows near $79) and reduced inflation/Fed hiking fears. The geopolitical relief trade is genuine and has partially repriced into the market (+0.56% in 24h). However, the bias faces a major headwind tomorrow: the FOMC under new Chair Kevin Warsh is expected to deliver a 'slightly hawkish' statement shift, potentially spiking real yields. OCBC strategists caution that gold's upside momentum may stall into the Fed decision and depends on sustained oil softness, lower yields, and evidence that hawkish Fed repricing has peaked. The move is already in Phase 2 (underway), so conviction is capped at 6.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC Decision + Press Conference (Wednesday 16 June, expected time ~18:00 UTC) — NEW Chair Kevin Warsh; hawkish surprise would spike real yields and reverse gold bid
US-Iran Framework Deal Details Release (Trump said possible by Friday) — further peace confirmation could extend relief rally, but fine print may reignite geopolitical uncertainty
Oil prices (WTI/Brent) — if Hormuz reopens and crude stabilizes below $80, inflation expectations likely lower, bullish for gold; if energy rebounds, hawkish Fed risk rises
Fed Funds Futures repricing into FOMC — watch for market shifting dot plot expectations; any hawkish shock will hurt gold
US economic data (jobs, inflation prints) — any hot surprises ahead of FOMC would reinforce Warsh hawkish pivot
screening.risk_warning: Gold upside is capped at 6/10 confidence because (1) FOMC decision tomorrow is binary and high-conviction hawkish surprises (e.g., dot plot hawkish reset) would immediately reverse the recent relief rally and spike real yields; (2) the move has already begun (+0.56% in 24h), putting gold in Phase 2, meaning the initial news-driven relief is partly priced in; (3) USD strength despite oil collapse suggests safe-haven demand for dollars may compete with gold; (4) if Fed Chair Warsh signals intent to normalize policy faster than market expects, gold could face sustained selling into higher real yields. Primary invalidation: FOMC dovish surprise or explicit rate-cut signaling would push gold decisively above $4,400.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already underway (Phase 2); avoid chasing current levels. Wait for pullback toward $4,300–$4,320 support for better risk/reward. Alternatively, wait for FOMC outcome (Wednesday evening) and re-enter on any dovish surprise or sustained real yield weakness. If Warsh sounds hawkish, reduce bias to NEUTRAL and look for fresh entry on weakness below $4,250.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: Treasury yields described as 'little changed' per US Top News as Fed begins two-day FOMC meeting. No fresh real yield shock yet; markets awaiting Wednesday decision.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy (FOMC)
factors.summary: Fed meeting underway (decision Wed June 18). CNBC Fed Survey expects no rate change but removal of easing bias in statement. High event risk caps conviction; outcome is key catalyst.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD 'little changed' vs EUR, JPY, GBP per FXStreet; tight ranges (18-40 pips across majors). No directional conviction yet; BoJ hawkish hike (25bp to 1.0%) provides mixed support.

factors.name: Oil Prices (WTI/Brent)
factors.summary: WTI crashed $3.70 to ~$77, down 5.9% over three days as US-Iran peace deal unwinds geopolitical premium. Brent below $80 for first time since March. Lower oil typically bearish for real rates and inflation expectations → headwind for gold.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran Deal)
factors.summary: US-Iran peace agreement announced Sunday; Trump signals deal details to be released soon, Strait of Hormuz full reopening expected by Friday. De-escalation of Middle East tensions reduces safe-haven bid for gold.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: US May import prices +1.9% (in line, prior revised +2.0%); export prices +1.3% vs +1.2% expected. Modest upside surprise but not dramatic. ECB's Lane notes inflation 'in the pipeline' despite Iran deal.

factors.name: US Economic Data
factors.summary: US housing starts fell to 1.177M (lowest since 2020) vs 1.430M expected, down 15.4% MoM. Single-family starts weakened. Signals growth concern; typically would support gold but offset by peace narrative.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO surge (+17% in three days), mixed tech action (Nasdaq -0.5%, semiconductors weak, financials/consumer strong). Risk-on bias from Iran deal and tech euphoria; equities outperforming reduces safe-haven demand for gold.

factors.name: BoJ Hawkish Rate Hike
factors.summary: BoJ raised rates to 1.0% (historic hike); USD/JPY anchored near 160 despite hike per MUFG. Yen weakness limits carry-trade unwind relief; supports USD slightly, headwind for gold.

factors.name: Gold Technicals
factors.summary: Gold holding above $4,300 per FXStreet (currently $4,344.30). Small +0.70% intraday gain but flat on 24h (+0.03%). No clear breakout; prior BUY6/10 readings from same session have not triggered confirmation.

screening.narrative: Gold is caught between conflicting forces: the Iran peace deal and cratering oil prices are de-risking the market and undermining the safe-haven bid, while weak US housing data and an imminent hawkish-pivot Fed decision (removal of easing bias) create uncertainty around real yields. The FOMC meeting Wednesday is the dominant near-term catalyst; markets are pricing limited cuts ahead, which would be bearish for gold. Risk sentiment has rotated to 'risk-on' (SpaceX euphoria, equities rebounding), further pressuring gold's safe-haven appeal. A move is in progress (not extended yet, 4h change only -0.39%), but direction remains ambiguous pending Fed clarity.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC decision & statement (Wed June 18, ~2pm ET) — removal of easing bias expected; watch for dot-plot dovishness/hawkishness
Powell press conference post-FOMC (Wed afternoon) — tone/forward guidance critical
Iran deal formal signing (Friday) — if goes smoothly, further supports de-escalation narrative bearish for gold
Weekly initial jobless claims (Thu June 19) — labor market data into softer housing print
Any Trump tariff/trade war rhetoric before/after Iran signing — geopolitical reversal risk
screening.risk_warning: Invalidating the neutral bias: (1) FOMC unexpectedly dovish (cuts signaled sooner/more cuts) → real yields crush, gold rallies sharply; (2) oil stabilizes/rebounds above $80 → stagflation fears resurface, gold reverses higher; (3) Iran deal collapses or new escalation shock → safe-haven bid returns explosively; (4) equity selloff / VIX spike → flight-to-safety lifts gold despite Fed tightening bias. Conversely, hawkish surprise + sustained risk-on could push gold below $4,300.
screening.entry_guidance: Do not chase; move is only -0.39% in 4h and lacks conviction. Wait for post-FOMC clarity (Wed evening) or a retest of $4,300 support with fresh momentum signal before re-entering either direction. Current zone ($4,340–$4,350) is indecisive.
